intro: Praxelis paucidentata, also known as bushy praxelis or few-toothed praxelis, is a perennial herbaceous plant native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which can help to alleviate symptoms of fever, wounds, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Praxelis paucidentata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Praxelis paucidentata is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and it is also used in the production of herbal infusions and topical creams, making it a valuable resource for natural health remedies.
